unit_of_work: Build Loop monorepo scaffold & tooling (commits 50ff2bc..HEAD on chore/build-loop-scaffold)
scope_note: Scaffold + tooling only; challenge features explicitly out of scope.
rationale: All four independent lenses returned pass with only info-severity findings; no error findings and no fail verdicts, so by the aggregation rule the gate passes and routes to verify.

findings.message: Acceptance criteria met: the four clean-clone gate commands pass, no placeholders remain, CLAUDE.md documents Build Loop + tech rules, git history is one-commit-per-step, README documents requirements. Scope correctly limited to scaffold/tooling.

Tests are meaningful and behavior-focused: be-dev health endpoint asserts 200 + JSON; jamstack greeting asserts output; Playwright e2e verifies heading text and zero accessibility violations. Each was shown RED before GREEN.
Code adheres to documented patterns: Hono app pure (no module singletons), Next.js without inappropriate 'use client', strict TS everywhere.
Accessibility testing wired via AxeBuilder in Playwright; e2e asserts zero violations on the home page.
html lang='en' set; Next.js metadata (title + description) exported for SEO.
Strict TS with hardened flags; Biome enabled for lint+format; no committed secrets (.env ignored); safe PORT default in be-dev server.
Root vitest passWithNoTests guard is documented and pragmatic; allows future repo-level tests without forcing them.
Auto-discovery globs (apps/*/vitest.config.ts, **/e2e/**/*.spec.ts) let new workspaces integrate without root config changes.
Biome replacing oxlint+Prettier is justified in plan.md/CLAUDE.md; a comment in biome.json would further aid clarity (optional, JSON has no comments).
README concise and accurate; single setup command; command table matches real package.json scripts.
No placeholders remain in .claude/skills or CLAUDE.md; all referenced commands exist as scripts.
Clean git history (one commit per step) with clear messages; pnpm-lock.yaml committed; .gitignore sensible; documented layout matches reality.
